title: Multi-domain web hosting
body: I remember that at the beginning of this *internet* thing, having your own domain was an expensive and complicated matter. You had to do tedious management and pay much more than now, both in the registration itself and in the *hosting*. There were and still are many free *hosting* services that in exchange for advertising or payment for your internet access, in most of them you had a folder within their root directory like *www.whatever.com/users/~yourname* which was somewhat tedious to pass on to your friends or clients. Some gave you a "subdomain" (*yourname.whatever.com*) with which you achieved a simpler *url*. But nowadays registering your own domain is easy, cheap and fast and it's normal that you end up having a few in your name, often scattered throughout the network in various registrars and *hostings*, and in the end managing all of them becomes somewhat complicated and expensive. A solution to save time and money is to put all your domains in a single *hosting*, one that accepts multi-domains like [CyberNeticos](http://www.cyberneticos.com/alojamiento-web-hosting.php "Multi-domain web hosting"), where you can place **all your domains in a single [web hosting](http://www.cyberneticos.com/alojamiento-web-hosting.php)** and manage them as independent domains.
